description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages prepare, test, triage, and deploy and workflow rules that run on merge requests, the default branch, or tags. Use a .use-ruby template that runs on ruby:3.2, installs bundler, runs bundle install with parallel jobs, sets BUNDLE_FROZEN true and BUNDLE_PATH vendor, and caches vendor/ruby keyed by Gemfile.lock. Define a setup-test-env job in the prepare stage extending .use-ruby, caching the ruby bundle, and publishing Gemfile.lock as an artifact. Define a specs job in the test stage that runs on a matrix of RUBY_IMAGE values (ruby:3.0, ruby:3.1, ruby:3.2, ruby:3.3), extends .use-ruby, and executes bundle exec rake spec; also define a rubocop job in test that caches .cache/rubocop_cache, needs setup-test-env, and runs bundle exec rubocop --parallel. Add a code_quality job using the code-quality component with the ruby cache and depends on setup-test-env, a dry-run:gitlab-triage job in triage that runs bundle exec rake build, gem install pkg/*.gem, and several gitlab-triage commands with $CI_PROJECT_PATH, a manual dry-run:custom job that allows failure, and include SAST, secret detection, dependency scanning, and semgrep jobs that all inherit the general rules; tag the jobs with gitlab-org and gitlab-org-docker as specified.
